---
id: cross-detector-transfer
order: 30
title: Cross-Detector Transfer
short_title: Cross-Detector Transfer
status: planned
summary: Measure whether learned detector representations transfer across experiments, detector geometries, and event modalities.
category: representation-learning
technical_area: transfer-learning
physics_area: detector representation learning
detector_type: Cross-detector
technical_method: Representation learning
track: Representation Learning
label: Open Dataset Challenge
datasets:
  - multi-source-cross-detector-records
metrics:
  - linear-probe-suite
metric:
  label: Linear Probe Suite
  goal: Maximize
  documentation: /documentation/metrics.html#linear-probe-suite
facts:
  - label: Category
    value: Representation learning
  - label: Detector type
    value: Cross-detector
  - label: Technical method
    value: Representation learning
  - label: Timeframe
    value: Protocol owner needed
  - label: Dataset type
    value: Cross-detector benchmark records
  - label: Challenge type
    value: Transfer benchmark
  - label: Technical problem
    value: Cross-detector generalization
links:
  docs: null
  baseline: https://github.com/youngsam/doraemon_site/tree/main/baselines/cross-detector-transfer
  leaderboard: null
bibliography: ../../bibliography.bib
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: mathjax
---

## Scope

This planned challenge will evaluate representation transfer across detector contexts. It is intended for methods that learn broadly useful detector embeddings, including contrastive and self-supervised approaches [@oord2018representation].

## Protocol needs

The final protocol should specify source datasets, held-out target tasks, and the probe suite used for comparison. A minimal transfer score can average normalized probe performance across target tasks,

$$
S_{\mathrm{transfer}} =
\frac{1}{T}\sum_{t=1}^{T}
\frac{s_t - s_t^{\mathrm{baseline}}}
{s_t^{\mathrm{oracle}} - s_t^{\mathrm{baseline}}}.
$$

## Leaderboard

The leaderboard is not open until the dataset mix and evaluation tasks are fixed. The protocol should explicitly state which detector family is the source, which target family is held out, and whether fine-tuning is allowed.
